summary: DHH announces the launch of Writeboard, a free collaborative text tool with version control and difference tracking. He acknowledges that critics will find it too simple, just as they did with Basecamp, Ta-da List, and Backpack. Rather than apologizing for minimal features, he frames deliberate simplicity as 37signals' core philosophy and competitive advantage. The post is a brief product launch that doubles as a manifesto for doing less.
key_points: Writeboard launches as a deliberately minimal collaborative writing tool with version control and commenting
Every 37signals product launch has been met with criticism for being too simple, which DHH views as validation
The company's explicit brand promise is simplicity, not feature richness
Products should help people get work done better today, not impress them with feature counts
money_quotes: It's also a whole lot less software than many people probably expected.
All our subsequent applications have been billed as too simple and too little by a choir of pundits at their launch.
We've never been in the business of wowing people with an amazing wealth of features.
So while we'll continue to proudly underwhelm people looking for more software and more features.
We'll be equally proud to please those who find the less we do, just enough to get their work done better today than yesterday.
key_insight: Consistent criticism for being too simple is not a failure of product strategy but confirmation that you're staying true to a philosophy of deliberate minimalism.
